mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) rising month-over-month signals robust consumer spending that drives corporate earnings and equity demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and SPX 20-day returns is <= 0.10, or the sign of the correlation is negative over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 8% from a current value of 7.3%, it exceeds a threshold historically associated with recessionary conditions, leading to forward negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as risk aversion spikes.
failure_condition: In the 12 months following any UNRATE cross above 8.0, the average SPX 5-day return is non-negative or the proportion of positive returns exceeds 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS=0.08%) combined with rising CPI (CPIAUCSL=233.3) creates negative real rates that suppress nominal discount rates and encourage equity allocation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < CPI yoy change and CPI yoy change > 0
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with positive inflation
failure_condition: Any 2-year walk-forward period where joint condition holds but average SPX 20-day return is ≤0, or where the linear combination fails to produce a significant positive mean (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) falling below its year-ago level signals a supply-side contraction that depresses corporate profits and equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This mechanism is regime-conditional: active only when INDPRO is in recessionary territory.
failure_condition: Over any 5-year span, when INDPRO yoy is negative, the proportion of SPX 60-day negative returns is ≤ 50%, or the average return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A stable or rising industrial production index (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing activity that supports economic expansion and boosts equity markets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as output growth sustains earnings momentum.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and SPX 60-day return is below 0.05 over a rolling 10-year window, or in-sample correlation is negative over 20 years.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 900 thousand units from a current 896 signals a weakening housing market that drags on consumer wealth and sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as housing-linked sectors underperform.
failure_condition: During any period where HOUST < 900, the average SPX 20-day return is positive, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates while consumer spending (RSAFS) remains high, the resulting composition signals diminishing labor income support for consumption, forecasting a slowdown that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS mom growth < 0.1% and RSAFS mom growth > 1%
failure_condition: Over a 5-year period, instances of joint condition (PAYEMS slowdown + RSAFS strength) yield average SPX 20-day return ≥ 0, or the difference from unconditional mean is not negative at 95% significance.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles (rising FEDFUNDS) combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL yoy > 2%), the direct discount rate effect and policy uncertainty cause negative short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week. This mechanism is regime-conditional: active only in tightening phases.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: In any 3-year window where FEDFUNDS is rising and inflation >2%, the average SPX 5-day return is non-negative, or the proportion of negative returns ≤ 50%.
